response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack and web developer with a strong track record, but he is fundamentally mismatched for this role. The single hardest requirement — native-level Japanese language proficiency — is entirely absent from his profile (he lists English, Romanian, and basic German). Beyond the language barrier, his experience is concentrated in web technologies (TypeScript, Node.js, Ember.js) and cloud infrastructure, with negligible exposure to C-based embedded systems, LAN-switch firmware, network security protocols (MACsec, 802.1X), or Linux kernel internals. The small amount of C/C++ work listed (NI LabWindows, a university audio thesis) is too dated and shallow to satisfy the role's embedded networking depth.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software engineering experience across multiple languages and platforms, demonstrating adaptability
Some historical C/C++ exposure at National Instruments and in academic projects, providing a minimal foundation
Strong software craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development) that would transfer to any engineering team
concerns: Zero Japanese language proficiency — a non-negotiable hard requirement for this role
No embedded networking, LAN-switch, or security protocol experience (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal, SNMP, NETCONF)
No Linux kernel internals or source-code modification experience; career is entirely web and application-layer
